briefing.headline: Twelve more Russians join EU's naughty list
briefing.oneliner: The sanctions carousel keeps spinning with fresh faces.
briefing.what: EU adds 12 individuals and 2 entities to Russia sanctions list. Asset freezes and travel bans for hybrid warfare activities.
briefing.who_cares: Banks holding Russian assets. Travel companies. Anyone doing business with newly listed entities.

briefing.worst_case: Criminal liability for sanctions violations -- expect prison time plus unlimited fines.

briefing.headline: Citrus industry gets three new bacterial nightmares
briefing.oneliner: Every citrus grower just became a disease surveillance officer.
briefing.what: New rules to prevent three Candidatus Liberibacter species that cause citrus greening disease. Annual surveys, contingency plans, and quarantine zones when detected.
briefing.who_cares: Citrus growers, nurseries, garden centres. Anyone growing Citrus, Fortunella, or related species commercially or publicly.
briefing.deadline: 1 Jan 2028 for quarantine zone surveys, 1 Jan 2029 for general surveillance -- time to hire plant pathologists.
briefing.implications: Annual disease testing. Contingency plan updates by December each year. Infected plants get destroyed within quarantine zones.
briefing.worst_case: Entire citrus operations shut down within 50m-3km quarantine zones until eradication confirmed.

briefing.headline: Toy safety rules get chemical makeover and digital upgrade
briefing.oneliner: Every toy maker, importer and retailer just got stricter chemical limits.
briefing.what: New EU toy safety regulation replaces 2009 directive with tougher chemical restrictions and digital safety requirements. Covers adaptive toys and internet-connected products.
briefing.who_cares: Toy manufacturers, importers, retailers selling to under-14s. Online marketplaces. Anyone making adaptive toys for disabled children.
briefing.deadline: 12 Dec 2027 -- 30 months for full compliance including chemical testing overhaul.
briefing.implications: Chemical reformulation of existing toys. New conformity assessments. Digital safety compliance for connected toys.
briefing.worst_case: Products banned from EU market for chemical non-compliance. Fines under General Product Safety Regulation framework.

briefing.headline: Carcinogenic formaldehyde compound gets EU approval anyway
briefing.oneliner: Brussels approves known carcinogen because alternatives don't work well enough.
briefing.what: Formaldehyde released from paraformaldehyde and 2-hydroxypropylamine gets approved for disinfectants, cooling system preservatives, and metalworking fluid preservatives. Classified as carcinogenic category 1B but no suitable alternatives exist.
briefing.who_cares: Industrial disinfectant manufacturers, cooling system operators, metalworking fluid producers. Professional users only -- consumer products excluded.
briefing.deadline: Entry into force immediate -- this is approval, not new compliance requirements
briefing.implications: Continued use of carcinogenic substance in industrial settings. Strict professional-only handling requirements.
briefing.worst_case: Worker exposure to known carcinogen in metalworking and cooling systems with EU regulatory blessing.
